subject: Computer Networking
title: JMAP — The Modern Protocol
description: A micro-lesson that makes JMAP — The Modern Protocol usable.

hook.text: IMAP is from 1986. JMAP is its modern replacement — JSON-based, efficient, and developer-friendly.

buildup.text: JMAP uses HTTP and JSON. No custom binary protocol. Web developers feel right at home.

discovery.text: JMAP syncs only changes. Instead of re-downloading folders, it sends a tiny delta. Way less bandwidth.

twist.text: JMAP handles mail, calendars, and contacts in one protocol. IMAP needed separate standards for each.

climax.text: Fastmail built JMAP and contributed it as an IETF standard. Adoption is growing but still early.

punchline.text: JMAP modernizes email sync. JSON over HTTP, finally.

quiz.question: What data format does JMAP use?
